steps.title: Use the axis of symmetry
steps.content: The vertex is $(-3,6)$, so the axis of symmetry is $x=-3$. The two $x$-intercepts of a parabola are the same horizontal distance from this axis.

steps.title: Mirror the known root across the axis
steps.content:
Other intercept $= 2h - r_1 = 2(-3) - \left(-\dfrac{17}{4}\right) = -6 + \dfrac{17}{4} = -\dfrac{24}{4} + \dfrac{17}{4} = -\dfrac{7}{4}$.
The other x-intercept is $\left(-\dfrac{7}{4}, 0\right)$. That is the correct answer.
